qa_pairs.question: What is the command to upload an image to OpenStack using the OpenStack client?
qa_pairs.answer: The command to upload an image is `openstack image create --file <path-to-local-file-image> --disk-format <format> <new-image-name>`.

qa_pairs.question: When uploading an image to OpenStack, what do the parameters `<path-to-local-file-image>`, `<format>`, and `<new-image-name>` refer to?
qa_pairs.answer: `<path-to-local-file-image>` is the path to the file containing the image you wish to upload from your local machine, `<format>` is the disk format, and `<new-image-name>` is the name of the image as it appears on the OpenStack dashboard.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if the disk format is not specified when uploading an image to OpenStack, and why is it important to choose the correct format?
qa_pairs.answer: If the disk format is not specified, the raw format is assumed. If your image isn't raw format, this will cause issues, so you must choose a format that matches the format of your image.
